comment_text.sn4.12:4.3: The ten powers of a Realized One are detailed at AN 10.21 and MN 12:9.1.

root_text.sn4.12:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.sn4.12:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavā mahatiyā parisāya parivuto dhammaṁ deseti.
root_text.sn4.12:2.2: “ayaṁ kho samaṇo gotamo mahatiyā parisāya parivuto dhammaṁ deseti.
root_text.sn4.12:2.3: Yannūnāhaṁ yena samaṇo gotamo tenupasaṅkameyyaṁ vicakkhukammāyā”ti.
root_text.sn4.12:2.4: Atha kho māro pāpimā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ gāthāya ajjhabhāsi:
root_text.sn4.12:5.1: Atha kho māro pāpimā “jānāti maṁ bhagavā, jānāti maṁ sugato”ti dukkhī dummano tatthevantaradhāyīti.

translation_text.sn4.12:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.sn4.12:1.2: Now, at that time the Buddha was teaching Dhamma, surrounded by a large assembly.
translation_text.sn4.12:2.2: “The ascetic Gotama is teaching Dhamma, surrounded by a large assembly.
translation_text.sn4.12:2.3: Why don’t I go and pull the wool over their eyes?”
translation_text.sn4.12:2.4: Then Māra the Wicked went up to the Buddha and addressed him in verse:
translation_text.sn4.12:3.1: “Why now do you roar like a lion?
translation_text.sn4.12:3.2: You’re so self-assured in the assembly!
translation_text.sn4.12:3.3: For there is someone who’ll wrestle with you,
translation_text.sn4.12:3.4: so why do you imagine you’re the victor?”
translation_text.sn4.12:4.3: The Realized One, attained to power,
translation_text.sn4.12:4.4: has crossed over clinging to the world.”
translation_text.sn4.12:5.1: Then Māra the Wicked, thinking, “The Buddha knows me! The Holy One knows me!” miserable and sad, vanished right there.
